results.name: Palantir Technologies
keyThemes: Government surveillance and civil liberties concerns
Political associations and Peter Thiel controversy
results.topBullish: Palantir shares are surging after DA Davidson upgraded the stock to Buy with a $175 target, citing the Nvidia partnership and Michael Burry trimming his short position. 📈 #Palantir #Nvidia #Stocks
results.topBearish: Palantir will data mine NHS patient documents. They will then sell that to private healthcare corps & pharma companies who will in turn ramp up their costs charges and prices accordingly, extorting the maximum profit from illness. People WILL die.
results.summary: Social media sentiment around $PLTR is predominantly negative, driven by intense backlash over the NHS data partnership, surveillance overreach fears, and politically charged associations with Peter Thiel and government agencies. The few bullish signals are grounded in fundamentals — a DA Davidson upgrade to Buy with a $175 price target citing the Nvidia partnership — but these are heavily outnumbered by reputational and ethical concerns that could weigh on institutional sentiment and UK/European market expansion.

keyThemes: Posts unrelated to Affirm Holdings stock
One post references Affirm as payment method only
results.summary: None of the 47 analyzed posts contain meaningful financial sentiment regarding Affirm Holdings ($AFRM) as a publicly traded company. The overwhelming majority of posts use the word 'affirm' in colloquial, spiritual, or motivational contexts entirely unrelated to the BNPL company, with only one post referencing Affirm as a merchant payment option — insufficient to draw any investable signal.
results.name: Affirm Holdings
iShares Bitcoin ETF
keyThemes: Institutional accumulation and dominant ETF market share
Bitcoin supply scarcity narrative driving demand
IBIT vs MSTR as preferred BTC exposure vehicle
ETF inflow recovery after outflow period
results.topBullish: Bitcoin ETFs saw $1.93B in trading volume. $IBIT led the charge with $1.62B. FBTC followed at $227M, while ARKB traded $44.35M. This level of concentrated activity indicates where institutional attention is heavily focused.
results.topBearish: Recent monthly flows show outflows from spot Bitcoin ETFs and Ethereum ETFs, while leveraged MicroStrategy ETF demand has also faded. The crypto ETF bid is no longer one-way. $BTC $ETH $MSTR $IBIT $FBTC #Crypto #Bitcoin
results.summary: IBIT continues to dominate Bitcoin ETF market share with overwhelming trading volume concentration (~84% of total BTC ETF volume at $1.62B), and sentiment is broadly constructive with retail and institutional participants favoring it as the primary BTC exposure vehicle over alternatives like MSTR. Headwinds include recent short-term outflow episodes, bearish options interest from a subset of users, and macro-level concerns that the crypto ETF bid is no longer unidirectional.

results.name: Amazon
keyThemes: Long-term investor confidence in big tech basket
AWS and AI capex concerns vs. growth
Smart home and voice assistant competition
results.topBullish: Long-term investors continue to favor $AAPL $MSFT $NVDA $AMZN $GOOGL $TSLA for their proven track records and future growth potential across multiple technology domains.
results.topBearish:
@sreed1988EE @DudeWhoInvests **sreed1988EE** Amazon stock has solid revenue/earnings growth, but lagged due to:

- Massive ~$200B 2026 capex (heavy on AI/data centers) delaying near-term margin expansion & FCF.
- Caution on AI ROI timing vs. peers showing clearer monetization.
- AWS growth strong (~18-20%)
results.summary: Sentiment toward $AMZN is mildly positive but largely dominated by noise — most posts reference Amazon Prime Video as a streaming platform rather than express investment conviction. Among the genuinely investment-focused posts, bulls cite long-term positioning across AI, cloud, and e-commerce, while the primary bear thesis centers on near-term margin compression from heavy AI/data center capex and uncertainty around AWS monetization timelines.

results.name: Keelvar
keyThemes: AI infrastructure and power asset narrative
Technical support levels and rebound potential
Margin pressure and forced selling thesis
Retail-driven social media hype with WhatsApp group coordination
results.topBullish: Leopold Aschenbrenner turned $250M into $6B bought $BE in 2025 at less than $100. Remember, AI's bottleneck isn't just $NVDA chips and $MU memory. It's also power, land and grid access. These 3 stocks have the exact same set-up: 1. $KEEL target $50 in 2-3 years
results.topBearish: $KEEL suffers core small-cap infrastructure headwinds: slow project award cadence, thin recurring revenue, and heavy field operation costs that eat into margins. With almost no institutional long-term holders, every price surge only lasts as long as retail social media hype. Once https://t.co/GUKsMYcxlF
results.summary: Social media sentiment around $KEEL is overwhelmingly bullish, driven by a dip-buying narrative framing recent price declines as a forced-selling opportunity within the neocloud and AI power infrastructure theme, with price targets ranging from $20 to $50. However, the post composition raises significant red flags: multiple coordinated WhatsApp group recruitment posts suggest organized retail promotion, the lone bearish post credibly highlights thin recurring revenue, negligible institutional ownership, and margin headwinds that undermine the bull case's durability.

results.topBearish:

results.summary: Retail and semi-institutional sentiment around APLD is overwhelmingly bullish, with the stock repeatedly cited as a discounted entry into the AI infrastructure and neocloud theme alongside peers like CRWV, NBIS, and IREN, with price targets ranging from $58 to $82 against a ~$33 current price. The only cautionary note is a technical rejection at horizontal resistance, though even that post frames the Gann Arc support as a potential springboard, leaving the overall signal firmly in buy territory.

results.name: Iris Energy
keyThemes: Anthropic Australia data center deal speculation (1.4GW / $21.6B)
Significant price drawdown from highs (~45% correction)
Management and dilution concerns (RSU package, ATM)
800MW Bundey South Australia site as strategic asset
results.topBullish:
Why $IREN Fits Perfectly
•  IREN's Australian Project: In early June 2026, IREN announced its first Australian data center campus — an 800MW site in Bundey, South Australia (near Adelaide). This is one of the largest announced in the APAC region. They secured a transmission https://t.co/Rb6YhkZnsa
results.topBearish:
$IREN is down over 35% in a month and there's a lot of chatter over an Anthropic rumor that hasn't even been confirmed.

Meanwhile the numbers say net losses the last two quarters, an $800M pay package, and a $6B ATM still running. Not for me thanks
results.summary: Sentiment on $IREN is cautiously bullish, driven primarily by speculative excitement around leaked Anthropic documents suggesting 1.4GW of Australian data center demand — a thesis that maps directly onto IREN's secured 800MW Bundey site — though no deal has been confirmed. Bears highlight real fundamental concerns including consecutive quarterly net losses, an aggressive $800M management RSU package perceived as non-performance-based, and a $6B at-the-money equity offering that signals ongoing dilution risk, tempering conviction despite the compelling infrastructure positioning narrative.

results.name: Tesla
keyThemes: FSD regulatory approval push in EU
Optimus production line installation at Fremont
Strong Q2 EV delivery growth (+25% YoY)
results.topBullish: Miami becoming the 5th city with $TSLA Robotaxi service isn't what I'm watching. I'm watching how many still call it 'hype' as new cities go live. At what point does the market stop pricing promises and start pricing a network? Where do you draw the line? #Tesla #Robotaxi
results.topBearish: Tesla's Best-Ever Q2 Landed — and the Stock Fell
results.summary: Sentiment around TSLA is moderately bullish, driven by tangible catalysts including Robotaxi network expansion, Optimus production line initiation, record Q2 deliveries boosted by rising gas prices, and international market wins such as Taiwan's top imported EV ranking. The primary bearish signal is the market's muted stock reaction to strong Q2 results, suggesting elevated expectations may already be priced in, while a significant portion of posts are spam or noise that reduces overall signal quality.
